title: Smoky Crisp Russian Salmon Pilaf
description: A smoky, crisp, and adventurous Russian-inspired dish featuring salmon, temper, and low-sodium-friendly ingredients. Perfect for a flavorful dinner or lunch.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the crispy-fried onion and cook until slightly caramelized (3-4 minutes).
Add the diced fennel and cook until it starts to soften (4-5 minutes).
Add the salmon fillet and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Remove the salmon from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the skillet and stir in the smoked paprika.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet and stir to coat with the paprika mixture.
Add the lemon juice and salt-free seasoning to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the cooked salmon to the skillet and stir to combine with the rice mixture.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving platter and garnish with fresh dill.
